rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JOHN BERTRAND & VIRGINIA DAVES HOOK REVOCABLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/18/21 to be heard with the related case. Whenever originals are E-filed, the attorney or the fiduciary who retains the original must file a declaration certifying that the original document(s) will be retained in the same way as documents are retained under Local Rule 2.11 M.4., which provides that the original(s) must be retained "for at least the period after the termination, including all appeals, of the case required for conventionally filed documents." If the ending balance shown in the accounting differs from the bank statement, a reconciliation must be shown. [LRSF 14.35 D.] Attorney costs must show the payee AND the full description (e.g., what was filed or served), not just the payee. (Only the statement(s) verifying the ending balance is/are required for interim and final accounts. [Probate Code Section 2620(c)(2).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SERGE JOHN WHITE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/20/2021. No proof of service showing notice of the amended petition was given to Natalie Zieber and Katherine White is on file. Although notice was given to their attorney of record, no waiver or directive that notice to their attorney is sufficient appears to have been filed. Also, paragraph 5 of the attorney's declaration filed on 03/08/2021 did not address the issue of 1/3 of the decedent's separate property passing to the surviving spouse's estate without administration under Probate Code section 13500. No election has been filed by the personal representative of the surviving spouse's estate pursuant to section 13502, nor has the personal representative petitioned for relief under section 13502.5, to probate the surviving spouse's share of the separate property.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DEBORAH ANN ARMSTRONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Attorney is requesting $1,750.00 in extraordinary compensation. This request for $1,750.00 was denied in both Estate of Cleonia Kemp and Estate of Louis Armstrong. Unclear why attorney did not withdraw the request when the Amended petition was filed. 2) Attorney filed an Ex Parte Petition to Withdraw Funds From A Blocked Account but has not followed local rules regarding ex parte petitions. [LRSF 14.11] 3) Attaching a copy of a proposed order to a petition or supplement and telling the court to use that order is not proper procedure. Please drop off a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 before the hearing date.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOUISE DOROTHY ZINGRONE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine why no courtesy copy of the "report of status" petition has been provided yet (including the proof of service and a proposed order). [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7 A., B., and C.] Regardless of the trial -- or because of the trial -- the other beneficiaries have a right to know what the status of administration is and that they have a right to petition for an accounting. [Probate Code Section 12201(b).] A proof of service attached to a petition is NOT the proper form of notice for Probate petitions. Due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order on the courtâs website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/27/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7].

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GEORGE MATRANGA TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/11/2021. Attorney Amanda Ebey filed and gave notice approximately 4 months after filing the petition on the incorrect judicial council form DE-120. 30 days notice is required on the mandatory judicial council form DE-115. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7].

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHUI MAN LY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Hearing issues have been e-mailed to petitioner. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSE RAUL COLINDRES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required in light of Raul Colindres' potential objection to petitioner's appointment indicated in attorney's declaration. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELWOOD GEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/4/21. No proof of service. No proof of publication. No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A.] It also appears that the wrong will was lodged and attached to this petition, as the decedent is Elwood Gee but the will is Shu Quing Gee's. The petition caption is incomplete as to IAEA authority. Item 2b of the petition is incomplete as to the name of the person to be appointed. Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. If the decedent was survived by a stepchild or foster child or children who would have been adopted but for a legal barrier, they must be listed in item 8 of the petition. This Court also requires the Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) to be filed separately and confidentially. It appears that an amended petition must be filed. If an amended petition cannot be filed in time to mail and publish notice before the continued hearing (5/4/21), Counsel should contact the assigned Examiner to request additional time. A courtesy copy of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/26/21 for the 5/4/21 hearing.
